bottom_line: NRR 109% is genuinely good and the cross-sell into our base is real upside, but coverage is under 3x, win rate is softening, and discount creep + customer concentration say the standalone revenue is less durable than the headline. GAP — fixable, but priced as if it's already fixed.

pipeline.read: under 3x entering the quarter = already forecasting a miss on the standalone plan
pipeline.top_leaking_stage: enterprise eval — lost on 'no SSO/SCIM' and 'single-region'

retention_decomposition.read: 109% NRR on 88% GRR means expansion is masking real logo churn (12%/yr); a 109% NRR with 88% GRR is weaker than it looks

discount_discipline.trend: creeping up from 9% a year ago
discount_discipline.read: discount creep is the leading indicator of pricing/positioning weakness — competitive pressure is showing up in price

source_mix.read: no single source >80%, healthy mix; partner motion is underbuilt and is where WE can add the most

concentration_risk.read: top customer at 9% renewing in 7 months with change-of-control consent is a single-point revenue risk the board will flag

cross_sell_upside.dependency: requires SSO/SCIM + multi-region (the CTO P1 items) — revenue synergy is gated on the tech-debt fix
cross_sell_upside.read: the bull revenue case and the CTO's critical-path debt are the SAME bet

reasoning_technique: Chain of Thought — pipeline math explicit, question anything above historical averages
conditions: Underwrite the synergy case on GRR 88%, not NRR 109%
Confirm top-customer renewal + change-of-control consent before close
Hold cross-sell ARR credit until SSO/SCIM ships (gated on CTO workstream)
source: cro-review (six CRO forcing questions: pipeline coverage, win-rate trajectory, NRR decomposition, ramp time, discount discipline, source mix) + cro-advisor (NRR/GRR waterfall, magic number, concentration >15% flag, expansion share)
